description: Comprehensive reference for Fracktal Works 3D printer electronics -- BTT Manta boards, STM32H723, RP2040 CAN toolhead, TMC drivers, power supply, and Klipper MCU diagnostics

mainboard.role: Primary Klipper MCU + USB-to-CAN bus bridge

toolhead_mcu.can_impl: can2040 software CAN via PIO (Programmable I/O)
toolhead_mcu.flash_method: UF2 USB drag-drop or CanBoot via CAN bus

power_supply.logic: 5V DC for Raspberry Pi and logic

can_bus.description: CAN bus between STM32H723 bridge and RP2040 toolhead

common_issues.cause: USB CAN adapter disconnected or driver not loaded
common_issues.fix: Check lsusb for OpenMoko CAN adapter. Replug USB.

common_issues.cause: Bus termination missing or wiring issue
common_issues.fix: Ensure 120ohm termination at both ends of CAN bus.

common_issues.symptom: Toolhead MCU not found by canbus_query
common_issues.cause: RP2040 not flashed with CAN firmware or wrong CAN frequency
common_issues.fix: Reflash RP2040 with CAN bus Klipper firmware. Verify 500kbps frequency.

tmc_driver_errors.description: TMC stepper driver status and failure modes

common_issues.symptom: Unable to read tmc uart register IFCNT
common_issues.cause: No 24V motor power or wrong UART pin config
common_issues.fix: Check 24V power to motors. Cycle power to reset driver. Verify UART pin wiring.

common_issues.cause: TMC driver overtemperature or current limit
common_issues.fix: Check DRV_STATUS for ot/otpw flags. Add cooling fan. Reduce acceleration.
Check 24V supply stability. Check for short in motor wiring.

common_issues.cause: run_current too low or stealthChop threshold too high
common_issues.fix: Increase run_current by 0.05A increments. Check stealthchop_threshold.

mcu_temperature.note: Add [temperature_sensor mcu_temp] with sensor_type: temperature_mcu in printer.cfg

stm32h723_manta.klipper_build: make menuconfig: STM32 -> STM32H723 -> 25MHz crystal -> USB to CAN bus bridge
stm32h723_manta.flash_sd: Copy klipper.bin as firmware.bin to SD card, insert, power cycle
stm32h723_manta.flash_dfu: Hold BOOT0, connect USB, make flash FLASH_DEVICE=0483:df11

rp2040_toolhead.klipper_build: make menuconfig: RP2040/RP2350 -> CAN bus -> 500kbps -> GPIO pins for RX/TX
rp2040_toolhead.flash_uf2: Hold BOOTSEL, connect USB, copy klipper.uf2 to RPI-RP2 drive
rp2040_toolhead.flash_canboot: python scripts/canbus_query.py can0 -> python scripts/flash_can.py -d UUID klipper.bin

power_supply_issues.cause: 24V PSU voltage sag when heaters engage
power_supply_issues.fix: Check PSU rating > total wattage x 1.25. Measure 24V under full load. Hotend ~40W, bed ~200-400W.
Run vcgencmd get_throttled. Use quality 5V PSU. Avoid powering Pi from Manta USB port.

power_supply_issues.cause: Ground loop between PSU and Pi USB
power_supply_issues.fix: Use single PSU or tie grounds. Tape 5V pin on USB cable to prevent back-powering.

power_supply_issues.cause: PSU unable to supply heater current
power_supply_issues.fix: Measure heater resistance. Verify PSU wattage rating. Check wiring gauge.

key_features: RP2040 CAN bus support via can2040 library (software CAN using PIO)
STM32H723 USB to CAN bus bridge mode (appears as both gs_usb adapter + Klipper CAN node)
TMC5160 SPI + TMC2209 UART driver support with continuous error monitoring
MCU temperature sensing for STM32H723 (0x1FF1E820/0x1FF1E840) and RP2040

build_notes.rp2040: Run at 200MHz (up from 125MHz in Klipper 0.13+). Uses arm-none-eabi- cross-compiler.
build_notes.stm32h723: 25MHz external crystal. USB to CAN bridge mode creates /dev/serial/by-id/... for Klipper + gs_usb CAN adapter simultaneously.
